commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: $6.57/bu (CBOT May 12-13). Up more than $1/bu since January — highest since June 2024. Up 5%+ on USDA tighter supply forecast. FAO Food Price Index at 130.7 — highest since Feb 2023. USDA forecasts food-at-home +2.4% in 2026.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Corn $4.50/bu (CBOT May 11). Near three-week low — faster US planting progress offset energy price pressure. Fertilizer costs forcing acreage reductions. July futures at $4.55.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: Soybeans $11.86/bu (May 1 CBOT). Soybean oil at historic highs — supported by biofuel demand as crude disruptions boost biodiesel.

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: ICE Sugar #11 at $0.224/lb (April 25). Up 6.7% from pre-war. USDA forecasts sugar & sweets +6.7% in 2026.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Vegetable oil price index at highest since June 2022. Palm oil (CPO June) RM 4,535/tonne (April 11, down 2.33% — five-week rally snapped). Indonesia CPO IDR 16,049/kg.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Arabica $2.83/lb (May 1). Down from 2025 peak of $4.41 — Brazil 2026/27 harvest projected +12% easing pressure. Down 27.5% YoY. Container rates still up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.usdaForecast2026: 2.5% food-at-home (USDA May update), but rising energy costs may push higher
groceryImpact.note: USDA forecasts food-at-home +2.5% for full year 2026. Farm wheat up 8.4%. Palm/soybean oil each +8%. Tehran wheat flour up 120% in one month, nearly 200% YoY. IRC operational costs spiked 50% due to war disruptions. If Hormuz persists through summer, could add 5-7 percentage points. Consumer sentiment at multi-year low. 318M face hunger globally (FAO).

fertilizer.detail: Urea up 47% since February (Yara CEO, May 1). Half a million tonnes of nitrogen fertilizer not being produced. Yara warns Iran conflict could put 10 billion meals per week at risk from fertilizer shortages. Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. USDA projects 3.6% food price rise in 2026.

context: Day 75 5:10 PM PT May 12: FAO Food Price Index at 130.7 — highest since Feb 2023 (+1.6% m/m, 3rd consecutive increase). Vegetable Oil Index 193.9 (highest since Jul 2022, +5.9%). USDA forecasts food-at-home +2.4% in 2026. Wheat $6.57/bu (+16.9% from pre-war) — highest since June 2024. Corn $4.50/bu (+4.4%). Soybeans $12.09/bu (+19.5%). Container rates (Drewry WCI): $2,286/FEU (May 7, +3% WoW). Brent $110.43 (+38.3%), WTI $101.87 (+34.9%). Gas $4.504 (+47.5%). Hormuz Day 72. 76% of Americans cite grocery prices as leading affordability concern. Food inflation lag: 3-6 months.
